questions.text: Which portion of nephron is under control of ADH?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct because ADH acts on distal ends and collecting ducts.
It is not correct because ADH acts on distal ends and collecting ducts.

Antidiuretic hormone (ADH), also known as vasopressin, primarily acts on the distal convoluted tubule (DCT) and the collecting duct of the nephron in the kidneys. These segments of the nephron are responsible for regulating water reabsorption in the body. ADH increases the permeability of these segments to water, allowing more water to be reabsorbed back into the bloodstream, thus concentrating the urine and conserving water in the body.
It is not correct because ADH acts on distal ends and collecting ducts.

questions.explanation: Antidiuretic hormone (ADH), also known as vasopressin, primarily acts on the distal convoluted tubule (DCT) and the collecting duct of the nephron in the kidneys. These segments of the nephron are responsible for regulating water reabsorption in the body. ADH increases the permeability of these segments to water, allowing more water to be reabsorbed back into the bloodstream, thus concentrating the urine and conserving water in the body.

questions.text: What do we call the cell surface membrane of a muscle fiber?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: No it is not correct because it is called sarcolemma.
No it is not correct because it is called sarcolemma.

options.explanation: It is not correct because muscles are also included.
It is not correct option because muscles and glands are included only.
It is not correct option because glands are also included.
Effectors in the human body respond to stimuli. They include muscles, which contract or relax, and glands, which secrete substances in response to nervous or hormonal signals.

questions.text: B- cells of pancreas secrete hormone:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: B-cells of the pancreas secrete insulin, a hormone essential for regulating blood sugar levels by facilitating the uptake of glucose by cells. Insulin also plays a key role in promoting the storage of glucose as glycogen in the liver and muscles, thus helping to lower blood sugar levels after meals. Dysfunction of insulin secretion or action can lead to diabetes mellitus.
It is not correct because beta cells of pancreas produce insulin .
It is not correct because beta cells of pancreas produce insulin .
It is not correct because beta cells of pancreas produce insulin .

questions.text: Which disease is sexually transmitted?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct because tuberculosis is caused due to bacterium.

AIDS (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome) is a sexually transmitted disease caused by the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV). TB (Tuberculosis), on the other hand, is primarily transmitted through the air when an infected person coughs or sneezes, although it can also be transmitted through sexual contact in rare cases.
Dengue is not primarily a sexually transmitted disease. It is mainly transmitted to humans through the bite of infected Aedes mosquitoes. So it is not correct.
Cholera is not typically considered a sexually transmitted disease. It is primarily transmitted through the ingestion of contaminated food or water. So not correct.

questions.explanation: AIDS (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome) is a sexually transmitted disease caused by the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV). TB (Tuberculosis), on the other hand, is primarily transmitted through the air when an infected person coughs or sneezes, although it can also be transmitted through sexual contact in rare cases.

questions.text: Primary growth is due to to activity of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Primary growth in plants, which involves the increase in length of the roots and shoots, is primarily due to the activity of apical meristems. Apical meristems are regions of actively dividing cells located at the tips of roots and shoots. These meristems produce new cells that elongate and differentiate, leading to the growth in length of the plant. The continuous activity of apical meristems allows for the primary growth of plants during development.
It is not correct because primary tissue is added by the apical meristem .
It is not correct because primary tissue is added by the apical meristem
It is not correct because primary tissue is added by the apical meristem

questions.text: Start codon AUG represents the amino acid
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The codons for the amino acid serine are UCU, UCC, UCA, UCG, AGU, and AGC in mRNA.
The codon for the amino acid proline is CCU, CCC, CCA, or CCG in mRNA. Proline is unique among the amino acids in that its side chain forms a ring structure. It is not a correct option.

The start codon AUG represents the amino acid methionine (Met) in the genetic code. In addition to being the start codon, AUG can also code for methionine within a protein sequence, serving as both the initiation codon and an amino acid codon.
The codons for the amino acid valine are GUC, GUU, GUA, and GUG in mRNA. Valine is a nonpolar amino acid. So it is not correct.

questions.text: During PCR how DNA double helix is separated
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: During PCR, the DNA double helix is separated primarily by heat, not enzymes. The denaturation step of PCR involves heating the DNA sample to a high temperature, typically around 94-98°C, which causes the hydrogen bonds between the two strands of the double helix to break. This results in the separation of the DNA into two single strands. Enzymes, such as DNA polymerase, are involved in other steps of PCR, such as primer annealing and DNA synthesis, but denaturation is primarily achieved through thermal cycling.

This results in the separation of the DNA into two single strands. Enzymes, such as DNA polymerase, are involved in other steps of PCR, such as primer annealing and DNA synthesis, but denaturation is primarily achieved through thermal cycling.

questions.text: Organs which are functionally similar but have different structures:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct because organs that are functionally similar but have different structures are known as analogous organs. questions.text: Zoological name of rhesus monkey is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct.

The zoological name of the rhesus monkey is Macaca mulatta, belonging to the genus Macaca and species mulatta. Rhesus monkeys are commonly used in biomedical research due to their genetic and physiological similarities to humans. They are native to South, Central, and Southeast Asia, and are known for their distinctive red-brown fur and distinctive facial features.

questions.text: The decline in thickness of ozone layer is caused by increasing level of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The decline in the thickness of the ozone layer is primarily caused by the increasing level of ozone-depleting substances (ODS), such as chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs), halons, and other similar compounds. These substances are human-made and release chlorine and bromine atoms when they reach the stratosphere. These reactive atoms then catalyze the breakdown of ozone molecules, leading to the depletion of the ozone layer.
It is not correct because nitrogen do no deplete ozone layer.
It is not correct because main reason is CFCs.
It is not correct.
